relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Ephraim arrives not with repair but with a threat to burn a household.
Jephthah answers from the memory of being abandoned in danger.
He says he carried his life in his palm and asks why kin now come as enemies.
An insult about belonging turns grievance into civil war.
The river crossings become checkpoints controlled by vengeance.
A single sound is made into a death sentence, and kin slaughter kin.
Jephthah's brief rule ends under the shadow of daughter and massacre.
His large household forms alliances across boundaries, while daughters remain more than the roles assigned to them.
Ibzan's seven years close in burial.
Elon judges quietly for ten years.
His story is compressed into tenure and burial.
Seventy descendants on seventy donkeys display a broad house of power.
Abdon is buried in contested hill country.

contemplative_note: Judges 12 shows how quickly threatened identity can weaponize speech. The river should be passage, but it becomes a killing gate; a syllable becomes a verdict. Mystically, Shibboleth warns every community against turning accent, vocabulary, doctrine, body, origin, or belonging into a test for who deserves to live.
